title: zfs: add idmapped mount support for rootless containers
body:
Apply idmapped mounts to ZFS storage layers when UID/GID mappings are
  provided. This translates file ownership through the user namespace
  mapping, allowing containers to perform chown operations that appear
  as root inside the container but map to unprivileged UIDs on the host.

  The overlay and btrfs drivers already support idmapped mounts; this
  brings ZFS to feature parity for rootless container support.

  This works in conjunction with ZFS's zoned_uid property [proposed
  separately to OpenZFS](https://github.com/openzfs/zfs/pull/18167) which delegates dataset operations to user
  namespaces, enabling full rootless podman/docker with native ZFS
  storage.

  Signed-off-by: Colin K. Williams colin@li-nk.org

Resolves https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/147

<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

> I think we should hold this PR anyway until the kernel feature is merged

@mtrmac @giuseppe : "it works on my machine" ; ]  Rebasing now  and if there aren't any apparent issues. Perhaps we wait until merge for a final rebase if necessary ?
@mtrmac @giuseppe the zoned UID feature was merged into ZFS: https://github.com/openzfs/zfs/pull/18167 . Then I believe this should be ready for merge.

I don't care about zfs, but if someone from the community brings this forwards I am OK to merge it.

Have you actually tested that this PR works?

It doesn't even advise itself as supporting shifting (`SupportsShifting`), so how is it even going to be used?

How are you avoiding to leak a mount?

Closing this PR. On investigation I found that my rootless ZFS setup has actually been working with stock upstream podman (5.8.1) rather than my custom build with this patch. The patch's code path is additionally unreachable through the naive SupportsShifting()=false default, so it could not have run even if installed.

The mechanism doing the real work is the zoned_uid kernel property (openzfs/zfs#18167, merged), which authorizes ZFS admin operations from user namespaces. Combined with upstream ZFS's existing FS_USERNS_MOUNT and FS_ALLOW_IDMAP flags, this enables rootless podman/docker on ZFS without any graphdriver changes.

If idmapped mounts on ZFS are wanted for use cases that don't involve zoned_uid, a correct implementation would need a real SupportsShifting(), separate mount path, and two-phase Put() teardown — but I won't be the one to write it since my own setup doesn't need it.

Thanks @giuseppe and @mtrmac for the review.
